task_completion.description: Candidate office buildings are filtered based on the specified criteria.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show filtering logic for building_type_id == 4, year_built between 1801 and 1989, non-residential sqft >= 50000, 0 residential units, and a valid sales price.

task_completion.description: The viability score and potential new units are calculated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells show calculations for normalized building age (using 2026 - year_built), inverse normalized value per sqft, a weighted average (40% age, 60% value), and potential units (floor of non-residential sqft / 1000).

task_completion.description: The top 50 candidates are exported to top_conversion_candidates.csv.

task_completion.description: The zone conversion capacity is aggregated and exported to zone_conversion_capacity.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code cell shows a groupby operation on 'zone_id' aggregating total units and candidate counts, sorted descending by total units, and exported to 'zone_conversion_capacity.csv', or the resulting CSV file is visible.

task_completion.description: A scatter plot of building age vs value per sqft colored by viability score is generated and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scatter plot is visible either in the notebook output or as a saved image file named 'conversion_scatter.png'. The plot must show building age on one axis, value per sqft on the other, and use a color gradient to represent the viability score.
